name: severino-vault-mcp
description: Local stdio MCP server for Obsidian-style operations vaults.

global_options.name: --fingerprint
global_options.help: Print a hash of the installed package's Python sources and exit. Compared against the source repo by `site doctor` to detect a stale install.

commands.name: doctor
commands.summary: Validate configured vault frontmatter without starting the MCP server.

args.name: --propose
args.help: Print starter frontmatter for markdown files that are missing it.

commands.name: prepare-writeup-publish
commands.summary: Run prepare_writeup_publish for a writeup slug and print JSON. Exits 0 if ok, 1 if blockers / missing slugs / unresolved refs. Wrapped by `site publish-writeup` in the operator's shell tooling.

args.name: --include-tag-usage
args.help: Include per-technology usage stats in the response.

commands.name: validate-writeup
commands.summary: Run validate_writeup for a single slug and print JSON. Exits 0 if ok, 1 if blockers / missing slugs / missing images / unresolved refs. The CLI face of the validate_writeup MCP tool; wrapped by `site validate`.

args.name: --draft
args.help: Tolerate the published / published_at blockers so a draft can be gate-checked mid-authoring (they become nits).

commands.name: list-writeups
commands.summary: Run list_writeups for a filter and print JSON. The featured filter sorts by featured_order ascending — the order the home cloud renders. Wrapped by `site featured` in the operator's shell tooling.

commands.name: technology-catalog
commands.summary: Run get_technology_catalog and print JSON: every slug, label, and featured flag grouped by section. Wrapped by `site tech` in the operator's shell tooling.

commands.name: validate-all-writeups
commands.summary: Run validate_all_writeups and print JSON. Exits 0 only when every (published, by default) writeup passes the gate. Wrapped by `site publish` as the slug-free pre-flight.

args.name: --include-drafts
args.help: Validate published: false writeups too (default: published only).

commands.name: writeup-dashboard
commands.summary: Return every writeup summary and validation result from one shared vault snapshot. Used by `site manage` for fast startup.

commands.name: apply-writeup-plan
commands.summary: Read a JSON writeup mutation plan from stdin and apply all scalar updates plus the complete featured order transactionally.

commands.name: reorder-featured
commands.summary: Run reorder_featured: move a writeup to a 1-indexed featured slot (0 unfeatures it) and renumber the list sequential 1..N. Wrapped by `site featured <slug> <slot>`.

commands.name: update-writeup
commands.summary: Update scalar writeup frontmatter fields via update_writeup_frontmatter and print JSON. Omitted flags leave fields unchanged. Wrapped by `site manage`.

commands.name: touch-reviewed
commands.summary: Set last_reviewed to today on a vault doc via update_frontmatter and print JSON. Exits 0 if ok, 1 otherwise. Wrapped by the drift guards (cf-dns / adguard / nginx / ts-acl) after a successful pull — a pull is a review, so the date moves.

args.name: relative_path
args.help: Vault-relative path, e.g. '02 Infrastructure/AdGuard/DNS Rewrites — homelab.md'.

commands.name: backfill-aliases
commands.summary: Set each folder-note's (`<folder>/index.md`) Obsidian `aliases` to its `title`, so `[[Title]]` resolves and autocompletes for notes whose filename is the non-unique `index`. Derived from `title`, so idempotent — safe to re-run to repair drift. Writeups are left alone.

commands.name: find
commands.summary: Run the section-scoped vault search and print the same menu JSON the MCP's find_runbook returns: ranked hits, each with its best-matching section (heading, slug, one-line summary) — never a body. The human/CLI renderer of the emit-once menu.

args.name: query
args.help: Natural-language query, e.g. 'renew the TLS cert'.

args.name: --limit
args.help: Maximum hits to return (default 5, capped at 25).

commands.name: read
commands.summary: Read one vault doc by doc_id and print JSON. With --section, return just that H2 span (the token-minimal path); without it, the whole body. Honors the sensitivity gate — restricted bodies are withheld (no interactive unlock on the CLI path).

args.name: --section
args.help: Section slug or heading path from a `find` hit. Omit for the whole body.

commands.name: task-list
commands.summary: The backlog board: every `doc_type: task` doc, derived from the index (project tasks under 01 Projects/<project>/tasks/ + the 07 Backlog/ cross-cutting bucket), filtered and ranked. Default shows live work (open + active); the thin `backlog` CLI and the Obsidian cockpit render this — the MCP is the one task brain.

args.name: --stale-only
args.help: Only stale (open/active, untouched past the window).

commands.name: promote-note
commands.summary: Promote a captured note (e.g. an 00 Inbox/ capture) into a task, preserving its body and deleting the source. The capture → task half of the inbox loop; used by the Obsidian promote command.

commands.name: update-frontmatter
commands.summary: Update fields in an existing vault doc's frontmatter (the one writer — doc_id is immutable). Enum + relation fields are validated against the schema. Used by the Obsidian relation editor so author-time edits can't dangle.

commands.name: task-reconcile
commands.summary: Re-home tasks into tasks/ (live) or tasks/done/ (closed) per their status — the idempotent tidy sweep for statuses edited by hand (a Base/Properties edit) that didn't move through task-move.

commands.name: task-projects
commands.summary: The task-project universe: every 01 Projects/<project>/ folder a task can be filed in, with its open-task count. The one owner of where a task can go — pickers (the Obsidian modal, the cockpit) derive from this instead of re-walking the vault layout.

commands.name: task-add
commands.summary: Author a new task file. With --project it colocates at 01 Projects/<project>/tasks/ and links related_projects; without, it files a cross-cutting task in 07 Backlog/. Schema-validated, written through the one atomic serializer.

commands.name: task-delete
commands.summary: Permanently delete a task file (for mistakes / junk only — finished or abandoned work should be task-move'd to done/wontfix, which keeps it queryable). Resolves a bare slug or the full id; refuses non-tasks.

args.name: doc_id
args.help: Task id or slug (task-foo or foo).

commands.name: task-move
commands.summary: Move a task to a new status (open|active|parked|done|wontfix). Stamps closed: on done, clears it on reopen; done tasks are kept so 'what shipped' stays a query. Resolves a bare slug or the full id.

args.name: doc_id
args.help: Task id or slug (task-foo or foo).

commands.name: hq-manifest
commands.summary: Build the Severino HQ manifest with the package's shared frontmatter parser.

args.name: --report
args.help: Print the full result (missing_frontmatter, duplicates, counts) as JSON instead of the manifest entries. Backs `hq doctor`.

commands.name: brief
commands.summary: Doc-side vault state in one payload: recent changes, docs overdue for review, and inbox backlog. The vault leg of the `brief` shell tool, which composes it with repo and writeup state.

args.name: --review-after
args.help: Flag docs whose last_reviewed is older than N days (default 180).

commands.name: schema
commands.summary: Emit the canonical frontmatter schema (enum sets) as JSON. Severino HQ commits this output and validates against it so the two systems share one definition.

args.name: --json
args.help: Emit the schema as JSON (the default).

args.name: --check-doc
args.help: Instead of emitting, verify that a human schema doc's enum lines (doc_type/environment/status/sensitivity) match the canonical schema. Exit 1 and print mismatches on drift.

commands.name: topology
commands.summary: Derive views from the topology inventory (02 Infrastructure/Topology/topology.json) — the SSOT for hosts, containers, DNS rewrites, and access rules. The hosts/DNS/diagram all derive from this one file; AI reads it via get_topology. Write/regenerate with `topology-write`.

args.name: --emit
args.help: summary: AI-grounding JSON (default). tables: the markdown body. doc: the full Topology.md build artifact. figure: a `brand figure` topology spec. schema: the declared inventory contract (canonical JSON HQ validates against).

args.name: --check-doc
args.help: Verify the generated region of a rendered Topology.md matches the inventory. Exit 1 and print mismatches on drift.

commands.name: topology-write
commands.summary: The validated write path for the authored topology inventory: validate topology.json and regenerate its derived artifacts (Topology.md + topology.figure.json) and the last_reviewed stamp. With --replace, first read a new inventory from stdin (validated) and write topology.json. Use this instead of hand-regenerating.

args.name: --replace
args.help: Read a new topology.json from stdin (validated) before regenerating.

commands.name: infra
commands.summary: Read structured infra datasets through the one registry (02 Infrastructure/_infra-datasets.json). With no id, list the catalog; with an id, read that dataset from its declared source (a JSON cache file, or a doc reference). The CLI face of list_infra_datasets / get_infra_dataset.

args.name: dataset_id
args.help: Dataset id (e.g. dns_rewrites, proxy_hosts, topology). Omit to list.

args.name: --refresh
args.help: Read live via the dataset's drift guard, falling back to the cache (flagged stale) if the system is unreachable. Default: cache only.

commands.name: infra-write
commands.summary: Write a reflected dataset's live state (normalized JSON on stdin) to its cache file, regenerate the doc's generated table region, and stamp last_reviewed — one atomic-per-file write. The canonical write behind a drift guard's `pull`.

commands.name: daily-write
commands.summary: Replace the generated brief region of a daily note (markdown on stdin): the marked MIRROR:daily-brief span only, leaving the free-capture area untouched. Creates the note from the daily-note frontmatter contract if absent. The write behind tools' `vault daily`.

commands.name: describe
commands.summary: Emit this repo's command surface as structured JSON: every subcommand, its arguments, and help, generated from the argparse parser itself so it can't drift from --help. The 'Code/guards' leg of emit-once — AI reads it, a TUI renders a command picker.
